highlight: Kintetsu’s luxury Shimakaze — double-decker observation cars, leather saloons and a café — glides to the pearl bays and shrines of Ise-Shima.

name: Laview
highlight: Seibu’s radical Laview, with a huge curved windscreen and yellow living-room windows, climbs from Ikebukuro into the Chichibu mountains.

name: Fujisan View Express
highlight: Steep little Fujikyu line to Lake Kawaguchi, its wide panorama windows framing Mt Fuji rising straight ahead.

name: Saphir Odoriko
highlight: All-Green-Car sapphire E261 with a glass-roofed lounge, curving along the Izu Peninsula’s cliffs and coves from Tokyo to Shimoda.

name: Yakumo
highlight: JR West’s new 2024 tilting 273 series threads the Hakubi line’s passes to Izumo, home of Japan’s oldest grand shrine.

name: Ibusuki no Tamatebako
highlight: Split black-and-white treasure box train that puffs a cloud of mist from its doors, hugging Kinko Bay to the hot-sand baths of Ibusuki.

name: Aso Boy!
highlight: Family sightseeing express with a panorama cab and play areas, zig-zagging the Tateno switchback into the vast Mt Aso caldera.

name: Watarase Keikoku Railway
highlight: Open-sided torokko trolleys wind up the Watarase gorge on the old Ashio copper-mine line, ablaze with wisteria and autumn maple.

name: ITX-Saemaeul
highlight: Tilting-comfort intercity that succeeded the storied Saemaul-ho, still running the full 442 km Gyeongbu spine from Seoul to Busan.

name: ITX-Cheongchun
highlight: Double-deck 180 km/h EMU on the Youth Line, racing up the Bukhan River to the lake city of Chuncheon, home of dak-galbi.

name: Mugunghwa-ho
highlight: Korea’s beloved everyman Rose of Sharon train — cheap reserved seats and rattling coaches taking the slow road from Seoul to Busan.

name: KTX-Eum
highlight: Home-grown EMU-260 bullet train on the rebuilt 250 km/h Jungang line, cutting Seoul to the Confucian old town of Andong to about two hours.

name: DMZ Peace Train
highlight: Tourist train relaunched in 2026, running to Dorasan — the last station on the line before North Korea, at the fenced edge of the DMZ.

name: Tze-Chiang Limited Express
highlight: Taiwan’s flagship Teng-Yun EMU3000, with a business-class car, curving down the wild Pacific east coast from Taipei to Taitung.

name: Taroko Express
highlight: Japanese-built TEMU1000 tilting train carving through the Yilan cliffs and tunnels from Taipei to the marble gorge country of Hualien.

name: Neiwan Line
highlight: Hakka-country branch line climbing from Hsinchu into the hills to the old timber and coal town of Neiwan, revived as a heritage day-trip.

name: Jiji Line
highlight: Taiwan’s cherished 1922 branch line through betel-palm country and a camphor-tree green tunnel, reopened in 2026 after a landslide closure.

name: Shen’ao Line
highlight: Short coastal branch to Badouzi, the Taiwan station nearest the sea, where rainbow-painted railcars run beside the wave-cut Shen’ao arch.

name: Raja Tehran–Tabriz
highlight: Iranian leg of the Tehran–Istanbul Trans-Asia Express, whose onward run once floated the whole train across Turkey’s Lake Van on a rail ferry.

name: Raja Tehran–Isfahan
highlight: Links the capital to the old Safavid capital and its UNESCO-listed Naqsh-e Jahan Square, shadowing Iran’s still-unfinished first high-speed line.

name: Raja Tehran–Bandar Abbas
highlight: One of Iran’s longest single-train rides: ~1,290 km across the central desert past Yazd to the Strait of Hormuz, completed when the Bafq link opened in 1995.

name: Yüksek Hızlı Tren (YHT)
highlight: Turkey’s second high-speed line (2011) cut Ankara–Konya from over 10 hours to under two, racing across the Anatolian steppe past salt-pan Lake Tuz.

name: Yüksek Hızlı Tren (YHT)
highlight: Turkey’s longest domestic high-speed line — 406 km over 11 tunnels and 6 viaducts — replaced a 12-hour conventional slog to Sivas with under three hours.

name: İzmir Mavi Treni
highlight: One of Turkey’s last classic ’Blue Train’ sleepers — 883 km overnight across western Anatolia via Eskişehir while the parallel high-speed line stays years off.

name: Doğu Ekspresi
highlight: Turkey’s cult 1,365 km trans-Anatolian sleeper to the snowy Caucasus frontier at Kars; social-media fame makes seats sell out weeks ahead.

name: Israel Railways · Beersheba line
highlight: A fast double-tracked run to the Negev’s capital, tracing the Ottoman military railway thrown down in 1915 to supply the WWI Sinai front.

name: Israel Railways · Coastal line
highlight: Israel’s busiest rail corridor hugs the Mediterranean shore from the Galilee coast through Haifa Bay to Tel Aviv, with trains every 15–30 minutes.

name: SAR East Train
highlight: Saudi Arabia’s first railway — the 1951 Dammam–Riyadh line built under King Abdulaziz — still crosses the Eastern desert and the vast Al-Ahsa oasis.

name: SAR North Train
highlight: At 1,242 km Saudi Arabia’s longest passenger line strikes north from Riyadh across the Great Nafud desert to Qurayyat near the Jordanian border.

name: Talgo Intercity
highlight: Cairo–Alexandria is the oldest railway in Africa and the Middle East (1854–56); since December 2022 it has been worked by Spain’s Talgo 230 trainsets.

name: Hejaz Railway (heritage)
highlight: Lawrence of Arabia’s Ottoman pilgrimage railway; heritage trains still run north from Amman via Zarqa, and a Hejaz steam loco fired up again in 2025 after 40 years.

name: Al Atlas
highlight: The everyday intercity linking Morocco’s imperial cities — Rabat, Meknès and Fès — up to eight times a day, parallel to but separate from the Al Boraq high-speed line.

name: Al Atlas
highlight: A surviving direct classic train after Al Boraq took the coastal corridor in 2018 — still running Tanger Ville to Fès with no change, via the Rif foothills.

name: Al Atlas Oriental
highlight: The slow ’Oriental’ run threads the Taza Gap between the Rif and Middle Atlas to reach Oujda on the sealed Algerian frontier.

name: SNCFT Grandes Lignes
highlight: Tunisia’s metre-gauge trunk line runs frequent air-conditioned expresses down the Sahel coast past the great Roman amphitheatre of El Jem to Sfax.

name: Accra–Tema Shuttle
highlight: Revived in July 2022, the shuttle turns a peak-hour road crawl of up to two hours into a roughly one-hour rail run between Ghana’s capital and its main port at Tema.

name: Sitarail Voyageurs
highlight: The lone survivor of the once-famous Abidjan–Ouagadougou train — a weekly Burkina-only service revived in November 2023 between the two biggest cities.

name: Transgabonais
highlight: Gabon’s only passenger railway climbs from the Atlantic at Owendo through equatorial rainforest past Lopé National Park to Franceville, running largely overnight.

name: Matadi–Kinshasa
highlight: Reopened in September 2025 after a five-year landslide closure, now worked at weekends by KiHa 183 railcars shipped from Japan, relinking Kinshasa to its Atlantic port.

name: Shosholoza Meyl
highlight: The last affordable long-distance sleeper in South Africa — an overnight economy train across the Free State Highveld and Stormberg to the Indian Ocean at East London.

name: Nampula–Cuamba
highlight: One of Africa’s great budget rail adventures — a full day across the baobab-studded bush of Niassa, carriages packed with traders and sacks of produce.

name: Sena Line
highlight: A subsidised lifeline up the Zambezi valley, crossing the 3.7 km Dona Ana bridge — once the longest railway bridge in Africa — from the port of Beira to the Tete coalfields.

name: Kampala–Mukono commuter
highlight: Revived in December 2024 on the century-old metre-gauge ’Lunatic Line,’ it beats Kampala’s notorious gridlock into the city for a few thousand shillings.

name: Fianarantsoa–Côte Est (FCE)
highlight: Madagascar’s rainforest lifeline — 67 bridges and 48 tunnels down a 1,100 m escarpment past waterfalls; villages survive by loading bananas and coffee onto the train.

name: Trans Lémurie Express
highlight: Reopened in December 2023 after ~30 years shut by cyclone damage, its rosewood-trimmed cars climb through the highlands to the spa town of Antsirabe.

name: Eritrean Railway
highlight: Pre-war Italian Mallet steam locos still claw up 3.5% grades and switchbacks 2,300 m between the Red Sea and Asmara — one of the great colonial mountain railways.

name: Port Sudan–Atbara
highlight: The first railway to reopen after Sudan’s civil war (December 2024) — a fortnightly desert lifeline from the Red Sea across Nubian scrub to the Nile junction at Atbara.

name: SJ Snabbtåg
highlight: SJ’s tilting X2000 links Stockholm with Malmö and the Øresund gateway at up to 200 km/h — Sweden’s flagship fast run down the Southern Main Line.

name: SJ Snabbtåg
highlight: Fast SJ trains climb the East Coast Line along the Gulf of Bothnia’s shore to the old timber city of Sundsvall.

name: SJ Nattåg
highlight: SJ’s sleeper rolls out of Stockholm at night and wakes among the snow-clad fells of Jämtland at the Åre ski resort.

name: Norrtåg
highlight: A rare passenger run through Norrland’s forests joining two northern university cities near the head of the Gulf of Bothnia.

name: Roslagsbanan
highlight: Sweden’s last commercial narrow-gauge railway (891 mm), still threading Stockholm’s leafy northern suburbs out to rural Kårsta.

name: Öresundståg
highlight: Rolls off Sweden’s West Coast Line and over the 8 km Øresund Bridge, plunging into the Drogden tunnel to reach Copenhagen.

name: DSB InterCity
highlight: DSB’s long haul up Jutland — crossing the Great Belt Fixed Link — to Aalborg, gateway to Denmark’s north.

name: DSB InterCity
highlight: Runs west to Esbjerg, Denmark’s North Sea port and the historic embarkation point for steamers and emigrants to Britain.

name: Østfoldbanen
highlight: Vy’s RE20 traces the Østfold Line down the east shore of the Oslofjord toward the Swedish border at Halden.

name: Gjøvikbanen
highlight: Climbs out of Oslo through the deep Nordmarka forest and over Hadeland to Lake Mjøsa at Gjøvik.

name: Vestfoldbanen
highlight: The Vestfold Line runs down the fjord’s west side through Tønsberg, Norway’s oldest town, to industrial Grenland.

name: VR Pendolino
highlight: VR’s tilting Pendolino sprints up the 200 km/h Finnish Main Line toward the head of the Gulf of Bothnia at Oulu.

name: VR InterCity
highlight: A double-deck InterCity east along the Karelian line to Joensuu, gateway to Finland’s forested North Karelia.

name: VR InterCity
highlight: The Savonia line threads north between the islands of Finland’s lake district to Kuopio.

name: VR Night Train
highlight: Finland’s northernmost railhead: an overnight car-carrier sleeper above the Arctic Circle to the fells of Ylläs and Levi.

name: Elron
highlight: Estonia’s busiest intercity run — orange Stadler FLIRTs linking Tallinn with Tartu, the country’s ancient university city.

name: Elron
highlight: Runs east to Narva on the Russian frontier, where two medieval castles face off across the border river.

name: Elron
highlight: A rural FLIRT ride south through Estonia’s forests and bogs to the lakeside hill town of Viljandi.

name: Vivi
highlight: The historic Riga–Dünaburg line to Daugavpils, Latvia’s second city, hard by the Lithuanian and Belarusian borders.

name: Vivi
highlight: Vivi’s new PESA railcars reach the twin border town of Valga/Valka — split between Estonia and Latvia — now a link in the Baltic capitals chain.

name: Vivi
highlight: A once-daily run reborn in 2017, crossing Courland to Liepāja, the windswept Baltic port and former Tsarist naval base.

name: LTG Link
highlight: Hourly express between Lithuania’s two largest cities along a segment of the old St Petersburg–Warsaw Railway.

name: LTG Link
highlight: LTG Link’s longest domestic haul, crossing Samogitia to Klaipėda, Lithuania’s only seaport on the Baltic.

name: LTG Link
highlight: The first direct Vilnius–Riga train in decades, restored in 2023, reconnecting two Baltic capitals via Šiauliai and Jelgava.

name: LTG Link
highlight: For years the Baltics’ only rail gateway to the rest of Europe — Vilnius to Warsaw through the Suwałki Gap, changing trains at Mockava.

name: Vande Bharat Express
highlight: India’s third Vande Bharat: a fully-AC self-propelled set gliding the Mumbai–Ahmedabad corridor at up to 130 km/h.

name: Vande Bharat Express
highlight: India’s second Vande Bharat, launched 2019 to carry pilgrims 655 km from Delhi to the Vaishno Devi base town of Katra.

name: Vande Bharat Express
highlight: South India’s first Vande Bharat (India’s 5th), linking Chennai, Bengaluru’s tech hub and the palace city of Mysuru.

name: Vande Bharat Express
highlight: Crosses the Chenab Rail Bridge — 359 m above the river, the world’s highest railway arch bridge, taller than the Eiffel Tower.

name: Gatimaan Express
highlight: India’s first 160 km/h train — the “Speedy” express dashes Delhi to Jhansi via Agra’s Taj and the fort city of Gwalior.

name: Vivek Express
highlight: India’s longest train journey: 4,154 km and roughly 75 hours from Assam’s tea country to the subcontinent’s southern tip.

name: Palace on Wheels
highlight: India’s original luxury train (1982): a week-long Rajasthan circuit in carriages styled on the private saloons of the maharajas.

name: Deccan Odyssey
highlight: Maharashtra’s answer to the Palace on Wheels — a blue-liveried luxury rake looping the Konkan coast to the Ajanta and Ellora caves.

name: Kangra Valley Railway
highlight: The world’s longest 2 ft 6 in narrow-gauge line, curling 164 km beneath the snow-capped Dhauladhar range on nearly 1,000 bridges.

name: Tejas Express
highlight: India’s first train run by a separate operator, IRCTC, rather than the Railways — with onboard hosts and automatic payouts for delays.

name: Duronto Express
highlight: An all-AC, near-non-stop “Duronto” linking India’s two biggest cities across the central heartland in about 27 hours.

name: Swarna Shatabdi
highlight: The Swarna Shatabdi day train (1994) speeds from the capital to the Golden Temple city of Amritsar in a shade over six hours.

name: Subak Raftar
highlight: A fast daytime intercity up Pakistan’s historic Main Line 1, sprinting Lahore to Rawalpindi across the Chenab and Jhelum rivers.

name: Parabat Express
highlight: Runs Dhaka to Sylhet through the emerald tea gardens and forested hills of Srimangal, Bangladesh’s brew capital.

name: Sundarban Express
highlight: Now races Dhaka to Khulna over the 6.1 km Padma Bridge, whose rail deck opened in 2024 to slash the journey to the southwest.

name: Banalata Express
highlight: Bangladesh’s flagship no-stop intercity, sweeping across the 4.8 km Jamuna “Bangabandhu” Bridge on its Dhaka–Rajshahi dash.

name: Yal Devi
highlight: Sri Lanka’s Northern Line express — its tracks to Jaffna, torn up during 25 years of civil war, were relaid and reopened in 2015.

name: Lào Cai Sleeper
highlight: Overnight metre-gauge sleeper up the Red River valley to Lào Cai, the mountain gateway to the terraced rice slopes of Sapa.

name: Hai Phong Line
highlight: French-built in 1902, this metre-gauge line still links Hanoi to Vietnam’s great northern port through the Red River delta.

name: Da Lat Railway
highlight: The last working stub of the French cog railway to Da Lat — 7 km of heritage track from a 1938 Art Deco station to Trại Mát.

name: Maeklong Railway
highlight: At Mae Klong the train noses through Talat Rom Hup, the market whose stallholders whisk their awnings off the rails as it passes.

name: Eastern Line
highlight: Bargain 3rd-class ordinary trains rattle to the frontier at Ban Khlong Luek — Thailand’s rail gateway to Cambodia and Angkor.

name: KTM ETS
highlight: The world’s fastest metre-gauge service at 140 km/h, running the peninsula’s west coast to the Thai border at Padang Besar.

name: Jungle Railway
highlight: The nicknamed Jungle Railway threads Malaysia’s rainforested interior past Taman Negara, from Gemas up to Tumpat near the Thai border.

name: Argo Parahyangan
highlight: The scenic conventional climb over the Priangan highlands and tall Cikubang viaduct — the leisurely rival to the Whoosh bullet line.

name: KRL Commuterline
highlight: Java’s first electrified inter-city commuter outside Jakarta, gliding past the 9th-century Prambanan temples between two royal cities.

name: Yangon–Mandalay Express
highlight: Myanmar’s 1889 British-built rail spine, now covered in about 10 hours by new air-conditioned DEMU expresses.

name: PNR Commuter
highlight: A surviving stretch of the century-old Manila–Legazpi main line, its ex-Japanese railcars trundling through Quezon’s coconut country.

name: Cornish Riviera Express
highlight: The daytime flyer to Britain’s far south-west since 1904, racing 300 miles past Dartmoor and along the wave-battered Dawlish sea wall to Penzance.

name: South Wales Main Line
highlight: GWR’s flagship South Wales express; Hitachi bi-modes drop off the 25 kV wires west of Cardiff and switch to diesel for the last dash to Swansea.

name: Great Western Main Line
highlight: Brunel’s original 1841 main line — famously flat and straight, boring dead-level through the 3 km Box Tunnel, said to face the sunrise on his birthday.

name: Avanti West Coast
highlight: Italian-built tilting Pendolinos lean into the curves of the West Coast Main Line, holding 125 mph where an upright train would have to brake.

name: Avanti West Coast
highlight: Fast tilting Pendolinos finish at Liverpool Lime Street, opened 1836 and one of the world’s oldest grand terminus stations still in daily use.

name: Avanti West Coast
highlight: Traces the pioneering 1838 London and Birmingham Railway — the first great trunk line driven into the capital — from Euston to England’s second city.

name: LNER
highlight: Japanese-designed Azuma bi-modes sprint the East Coast Main Line out of King’s Cross (1852) to reach Leeds in a little over two hours.

name: LNER
highlight: Azuma expresses hold 125 mph down the East Coast Main Line, sweeping over the Tyne into Newcastle on Robert Stephenson’s High Level Bridge.

name: CrossCountry
highlight: Britain’s longest direct train — one Voyager grinding 695 miles and nearly 40 stops from Aberdeen on the North Sea to Plymouth on the Channel.

name: Kyle of Lochalsh Line
highlight: One of the world’s great scenic railways, blasted through Torridonian rock for 82 miles from Inverness to the Skye ferry pier at Kyle of Lochalsh.

name: Far North Line
highlight: Britain’s most northerly line, a lonely 4-hour run to Wick that swings far inland on the Lairg Loop to dodge the deep Highland sea firths.

name: Heart of Wales Line
highlight: A 121-mile request-stop ramble through empty Mid Wales, crossing the castle-like 13-arch Knucklas Viaduct where trains halt only if you flag them down.

name: Cambrian Coast Line
highlight: Clings to the Mid-Wales shore on mostly single track, rumbling across the half-mile timber Barmouth Viaduct over the Mawddach estuary.

name: North Wales Coast Line
highlight: The old Irish Mail route to the Dublin ferries, crossing Robert Stephenson’s Britannia Bridge to Anglesey and skirting the walls of Conwy Castle.

name: North Yorkshire Moors Railway
highlight: One of the world’s busiest steam heritage lines, climbing Stephenson’s 1836 route over the moors to Goathland — Hogsmeade in the Harry Potter films.

name: Severn Valley Railway
highlight: Sixteen miles of preserved Great Western branch line tracing the River Severn, crossing the water on the graceful iron span of Victoria Bridge.

name: Bluebell Railway
highlight: The world’s first preserved standard-gauge passenger railway, running vintage steam through the Sussex Weald without a break since 1960.

name: West Somerset Railway
highlight: Britain’s longest heritage railway — nearly 23 miles of steam from the Quantock Hills down to the Bristol Channel shore at Minehead.

name: Vale of Rheidol Railway
highlight: Narrow-gauge steam grinding 200 m uphill from the sea at Aberystwyth to the wooded gorge of Devil’s Bridge — the last steam line British Rail ever ran.

name: InterCity
highlight: Iarnród Éireann’s diesel railcars strike west across the boggy Irish Midlands, crossing the Shannon at Athlone to the Atlantic city of Galway.

name: InterCity
highlight: Leaves the Dublin–Cork main line at Limerick Junction, a curious station where trains long had to reverse, before the final run to the Shannon at Limerick.

name: InterCity
highlight: The old Midland Great Western route from Dublin Connolly over the drumlin lakelands to Sligo, W. B. Yeats’s country beneath flat-topped Ben Bulben.

name: InterCity
highlight: Curves south-west from Dublin through medieval Kilkenny to Waterford, Ireland’s oldest city, founded by Vikings on the tidal River Suir.

name: DART
highlight: Ireland’s only electrified railway, hugging Dublin Bay from the Howth peninsula past Killiney’s Irish Riviera to Greystones since 1984.

name: Belfast–Derry Line
highlight: Michael Palin called the Coleraine–Derry run one of the world’s most beautiful; the train skims the Atlantic strand below cliff-top Mussenden Temple.

name: Dartmoor Line
highlight: The first line reopened under Britain’s Restoring Your Railway plan, bringing regular trains back to Okehampton on the edge of Dartmoor half a century after closure.

name: Šargan Eight
highlight: A 760 mm loop climbing 300 m through 22 tunnels and 5 bridges in a literal figure-eight to gain height.

name: Belgrade–Niš Line
highlight: Serbia’s first main line squeezes through the Grdelica Gorge on stone viaducts — Corridor X’s slowest link, now rebuilding for 200 km/h.

name: EuroCity “Sava”
train: SŽ series 342 electric locomotive · EuroCity coaches
highlight: The EC “Sava” restored a direct Zagreb–Ljubljana run, and since June 2025 extends once a week onward to Pula for the first time in 30 years.

name: Bucharest–Constanța InterCity
highlight: Romania’s fastest domestic run crosses the Danube on Anghel Saligny’s 1895 bridge before new EMUs race on to the Black Sea.

name: Ionian Coast Line
highlight: Regional trains hug the Ionian shore beneath Mount Etna, calling at Taormina-Giardini before the Baroque port of Siracusa.

name: Tren de la Fresa
operator: Fundación de los Ferrocarriles Españoles · Renfe
highlight: Hostesses in 1920s dress hand out strawberries aboard replica carriages on Spain’s second-ever railway, opened in 1851.

name: El Canfranero
highlight: Reopened in June 2025 after two years of works, the line climbs to Europe’s grandest abandoned border station, shut since a 1970 bridge collapse.

name: R11 Costa Brava Line
highlight: Regional trains skirt Costa Brava cliffs near the French border before Portbou, terminus of the line completed in 1878.

name: Linha do Vouga
train: Metre-gauge diesel railcar · heritage wooden-carriage train (summer Sat.)
highlight: Portugal’s last surviving metre-gauge line still carries commuters — and a vintage wooden-carriage special every summer Saturday.

name: Ligne des Pyrénées
highlight: Four daily trains climb past Foix’s castle and Ax-les-Thermes to Latour-de-Carol, where three different rail gauges meet.

name: Montreux–Rochers-de-Naye Railway
highlight: Climbs 1,700 m from Lake Geneva’s shore to a marmot-filled Alpine garden in under an hour, on Abt rack track dating to 1892.

name: Wengernalpbahn
highlight: The world’s longest continuous cogwheel railway threads Lauterbrunnen to Grindelwald beneath the Eiger’s north face via Kleine Scheidegg.

name: Furka Cogwheel Steam Railway
highlight: Volunteer-run steam locomotives claw over the 2,160 m Furka Pass past the Rhône Glacier on track abandoned when the base tunnel opened in 1982.

name: Brienz Rothorn Bahn
highlight: A 25% grade steam rack line — Europe’s highest unelectrified railway — climbs from Lake Brienz to a 2,244 m summit in an hour.

name: Chur–Arosa Line
train: RhB Ge 4/4 II electric multiple unit
highlight: Seventy curves and the record-setting 1914 Langwieser Viaduct carry hourly trains from Chur up into the ski resort of Arosa.

name: Achenseebahn
highlight: Europe’s oldest still-working steam cog railway — its four 1889 locomotives haul open carriages up to the turquoise Achensee.

name: Schafbergbahn
highlight: Austria’s steepest steam rack railway climbs 1,190 m in 40 minutes to a summit overlooking eleven Salzkammergut lakes.

name: Pinzgauer Lokalbahn
highlight: A 760 mm-gauge valley line follows the Salzach past Hohe Tauern peaks to Krimml, ending beneath Austria’s highest waterfall.

name: Zillertalbahn
train: Modern diesel railcar · steam-hauled heritage train
highlight: A working 760 mm-gauge valley railway that still runs steam trains alongside its half-hourly regular service to Mayrhofen.

name: Čierny Hron Forest Railway
highlight: One of Europe’s last steam-worked forestry railways — its branch to Dobroč runs straight through the middle of a village football pitch.

name: Štrba–Štrbské Pleso Rack Railway
highlight: Slovakia’s only cog railway climbs 430 vertical metres up a 15% rack gradient into the High Tatras on hybrid EMUs delivered in 2021.

name: Trenčín Electric Railway (TREŽ)
highlight: One of Europe’s oldest surviving interurban tramways — 1950s narrow-gauge electric railcars still shuttle up to a Habsburg-era spa town.

name: Skagen Line (Skagensbanen)
highlight: Runs to Denmark’s northernmost station through dune plantations to the sand-blown artists’ town where two seas meet at Grenen.

name: Jokioinen Museum Railway
highlight: The last surviving stretch of Finland’s once-extensive 750 mm network, kept running by volunteers with steam engines built in Belgium.

name: Gulbene–Alūksne Narrow Gauge Railway (Bānītis)
highlight: The only regularly operating narrow-gauge passenger railway left in the Baltic states, rattling twice daily through Vidzeme’s forests.

name: Aukštaitija Narrow Gauge Railway (Siaurukas)
highlight: Weekend survivor of a 750 mm network that once ran 68 km to Panevėžys, now trundling summer tourists out to Rubikiai lake.

name: Carpathian Tram (Vyhoda Forest Railway)
train: TU8P / TU6D diesel locomotive · open carriages
highlight: Ukraine’s last working forest railway, built in 1890 to haul Carpathian timber, now carries tourists up the Mizunka valley past waterfalls.

name: Zaslonov Children’s Railway
train: TU2 / TU7 diesel locomotive · narrow-gauge coaches
highlight: A working 750 mm railway through Chelyuskintsy Park operated entirely by schoolchildren training as drivers, conductors and dispatchers.

name: Lillafüred Forest Railway
highlight: Narrow-gauge line through the Bükk mountains via two tunnels and two viaducts, opened for timber and now Hungary’s best-loved forest train.

name: Szilvásvárad Forest Railway (Szalajka Valley)
highlight: Hungary’s busiest narrow-gauge railway — a steep timber line up the Szalajka valley to a waterfall, now carrying 300,000-plus riders a year.

name: Jindřichův Hradec–Nová Bystřice Narrow Gauge
highlight: South Bohemia’s narrow-gauge survivor, rescued by a new operator in 2026 after the original company’s 2022 bankruptcy nearly ended the line.

name: Train 1900 (Fond-de-Gras Heritage Railway)
train: Steam & diesel locomotives (former Prince Henri Railway stock)
operator: AMTF (Association des Musées et Tourisme Ferroviaires)
highlight: Vintage steam trains rattle through Luxembourg’s old iron-mining Minett region on track first laid in 1874 to move ore and miners.

name: Bieszczady Forest Railway
train: Px48 steam locomotive · Lyd2 diesel locomotive
highlight: Poland’s highest narrow-gauge line, a former logging railway that now carries over 150,000 riders a year into the Bieszczady wilderness.

name: Waterford & Suir Valley Railway
highlight: Narrow-gauge trains follow the tidal River Suir on the trackbed of an 1878 mainline, running alongside the Waterford Greenway cycle path.

name: Sauschwänzlebahn (Wutach Valley Railway)
train: Class 52 / 93 steam locomotive · heritage diesel railcars
highlight: Germany’s only spiral railway tunnel — a 1,700 m loop at Stockhalde lets the line climb the Black Forest by circling back over itself.

name: Chemin de Fer de la Baie de Somme
train: 130T steam locomotive (Cail · Haine-Saint-Pierre) · Belle Époque carriages
operator: Association du Chemin de Fer de la Baie de Somme
highlight: Belle-Époque steam trains skirt the Somme estuary’s tidal salt marshes, with locomotives running round their own train at each terminus.

name: Chemin de Fer du Bocq
train: Vintage SNCB railcar · heritage diesel locomotives
highlight: Three tunnels and five stone viaducts pack into the Bocq valley on one of Belgium’s most dramatically engineered branch lines.

name: Museumstoomtram Hoorn–Medemblik
train: Preserved steam locomotives (SHM fleet) · vintage carriages
highlight: A steam tram dating from 1887 still crosses North Holland’s polders and tulip fields between Hoorn and the old Zuiderzee port of Medemblik.

name: Talyllyn Railway
train: Preserved 0-4-0T / 0-4-2T steam locomotives (Talyllyn, Dolgoch, Edward Thomas)
highlight: The world’s first railway saved by volunteers, in 1951 — its Victorian steam engines still cross Dolgoch Viaduct into the Fathew valley.

name: Romney, Hythe & Dymchurch Railway
highlight: Marketed as the world’s smallest public railway, its steam expresses still get local schoolchildren to class along Romney Marsh.

name: Selketalbahn (Harz Narrow Gauge)
train: Class 99.7 Mallet steam locomotive · heritage coaches
highlight: Metre-gauge Mallet steam locomotives still double-head trains out of Alexisbad on this Harz valley line, opened for timber and ore in 1887.

name: Arlberg Railway
highlight: Austria’s only east–west mountain mainline, vaulting the Trisanna gorge beside Wiesberg Castle before a 1,310 m summit in the 10.2 km Arlberg tunnel.

name: Al Ándalus
highlight: A rolling Belle Époque palace whose 1929 sleeping cars once carried the British court from Calais to the Riviera — now easing from Seville to Madrid in seven days.

name: Belmond Royal Scotsman
highlight: Just 40 guests aboard mahogany-panelled Pullmans that cross the Forth Bridge outbound and stable each night in Highland sidings.

name: TGV inOui
highlight: Paris to Barcelona in 6 h 39 min, diving under the Pyrenees through the 8.3 km Perthus tunnel at the Spanish border.

name: Berlin-Warszawa-Express
highlight: Six EuroCitys a day across the North European Plain, with the cult WARS dining car serving pierogi between the Oder and the Vistula.

name: Brenner Railway
highlight: The first railway over the main Alpine crest (1867) still tops the open pass at 1,371 m — no summit tunnel, just a station straddling the border.

name: Cremallera de Núria
highlight: The only way to roadless Núria: Abt rack rails climbing 1,000 vertical metres through the Freser gorges to a sanctuary at 1,964 m.

name: Lötschberger
highlight: The 1913 mountain line the base tunnel made optional — Felsenburg spirals, a 14.6 km summit tunnel, then the Bietschtal bridge on a ledge above the Rhône.

name: Intercity Notte Rome–Palermo
highlight: Europe’s last train on a ship: the sleeper is split at Villa San Giovanni and floated across the Strait of Messina before breakfast.

name: Frecciarossa Paris–Milan
highlight: Italy’s 300 km/h flagship on French rails, threading the 13.7 km Fréjus tunnel of 1871 — back since April 2025 after a 19-month landslide closure.

name: Tauern Railway
highlight: Franz Joseph’s 1909 crossing of the Hohe Tauern — stone viaducts up the Gastein spa valley to an 8.4 km tunnel at 1,226 m.

name: Isle of Man Steam Railway
highlight: Victorian Beyer Peacock tanks of 1874 still haul their original carriages down 25 km of 3 ft gauge to the island’s southern tip.

name: Coastal Classic
highlight: Hugs the bore-tide flats of Turnagain Arm, then climbs past Spencer Glacier into country no road reaches.

name: Belmond Hiram Bingham
highlight: Belmond’s navy-and-gold Pullman to Machu Picchu, with brunch, a pisco bar and citadel entry folded into the fare since 2003.

name: Train de Charlevoix
highlight: Runs 140 km wedged between Laurentian cliffs and the St. Lawrence, at times just metres from the tide.

name: Sudbury–White River
highlight: Canada’s last Budd RDC mainline train will flag-stop anywhere along 484 km of roadless Canadian Shield.

name: Winter Park Express
highlight: Amtrak’s revived ski train climbs more than 1,100 m through 28 tunnels and spills skiers out steps from Winter Park’s lifts.

name: Canyon Spirit
highlight: Crosses the Continental Divide inside the 10 km Moffat Tunnel, then follows the Colorado River two dome-car days to Moab’s red rock.

name: Strasburg Rail Road
highlight: Chartered in 1832, America’s oldest continuously operating railroad still steams through Lancaster County’s Amish farmland.

name: Mountaineer
train: EMD GP38 diesels, dome & open cars
highlight: Follows the 1875 Maine Central grade up Crawford Notch, creeping across the curved Frankenstein Trestle high above the Saco.

name: Royal Gorge Route
highlight: Threads the canyon won in the 1879 “Royal Gorge War”, the Arkansas River alongside and a suspension bridge 291 m overhead.

name: St. Kitts Scenic Railway
highlight: The last railway of the West Indies loops the island on a 1912–26 sugar-cane line, ridden in double-deck open-top cars.

name: Maria Fumaça de Tiradentes
highlight: Baldwin steam locomotives have worked this 76 cm-gauge line ever since Emperor Pedro II opened it in 1881.

name: Trem do Vinho
highlight: A 1941 steam engine ambles 23 km through the Serra Gaúcha wine country to a soundtrack of tarantellas and popping corks.

name: Blue Mountains Line
highlight: Climbs from near sea level to 1,017 m at Katoomba through a UNESCO-listed maze of sandstone cliffs and eucalypt canyons.

name: Savannahlander
highlight: Sixty-year-old railmotors take two unhurried days to cover 424 km from coastal rainforest to the 1910 gold-rush railhead at Forsayth.

name: Electric Tilt Train
highlight: The world’s fastest narrow-gauge train — it set Australia’s rail speed record of 210 km/h in 1999 and still tilts through cane country at 160 km/h.

name: Canberra Xplorer
highlight: The only passenger train to Australia’s capital — three Xplorer railcars a day take the 1914 branch past the wind farms above Lake George.

name: Cockle Train
highlight: Rides Australia’s oldest steel-railed public route, opened in 1854 with horse haulage to link Murray riverboats to the sea.

name: Te Huia
highlight: Restored intercity rail between Waikato and Auckland in 2021 after a 20-year gap — and went seven-days-a-week in 2025.

name: Southern Line
highlight: From Muizenberg the rails run on a False Bay sea wall so close to the surf that winter swells spray the carriage windows at Kalk Bay.

name: Nanyuki Safari Train
highlight: Crosses the equator on its final approach to Nanyuki, where Mount Kenya’s 5,199 m massif fills the carriage windows.

name: Luanda–Malanje Express
highlight: Angola’s oldest railway, silenced for decades by civil war, reopened end-to-end in 2011 after a Chinese-led rebuild of all 424 km.

name: Van Gölü Ekspresi
highlight: Ends 1,267 km from Ankara at a ferry slip on Lake Van, where wagons bound for Iran are floated across Turkey’s largest lake.

name: HaEmek (Jezreel Valley) Line
highlight: Rebuilt on the 1905 Ottoman Hejaz alignment, it ends at Beit She’an — about 120 m below sea level, among the world’s lowest stations.

name: Tehran–Shiraz Night Train
highlight: Completed only in 2009, the overnight run leaves Tehran at dusk and descends at dawn past Marvdasht, the plain of Persepolis, into Shiraz.

name: North Borneo Railway
train: Wood-fired 1954 Vulcan 2-6-2 steam locomotive, colonial-green carriages
highlight: A 1954 Vulcan Foundry engine burning jungle firewood retraces the 1896 British North Borneo line — the only passenger railway corridor on Borneo.

name: Araku Vistadome
train: Glass-roofed Vistadome coach on the Visakhapatnam–Kirandul train
highlight: India’s first Vistadome coach (2017) climbs the Eastern Ghats to Shimiliguda, long the country’s highest broad-gauge station at 996 m.

name: Sethu Express (Boat Mail)
train: ICF sleeper rake behind WAP-4 electric
highlight: Heir to the 1914 Boat Mail that fed the Ceylon ferry, it now reaches Rameswaram island over India’s first vertical-lift sea bridge, opened 2025.

name: Marine Liner
train: JR 5000 series + 223-5000 series EMU with double-deck panorama car
highlight: Every half hour it rides the lower deck of the 13.1 km Great Seto Bridge — the Seto Inland Sea’s only rail crossing, strung across five islands since 1988.

name: Sanriku Railway Rias Line
highlight: Japan’s longest third-sector railway threads 163 km of tsunami-rebuilt coast; the final gap closed in 2019, eight years after the 2011 wave.

name: Tsugaru Stove Train
train: 1948 Ohafu 33 coaches with coal daruma stoves, DD350 diesel
highlight: Aboard Japan’s northernmost private railway, attendants grill squid on pot-belly coal stoves while blizzards bury the Tsugaru plain outside.

name: Hue–Da Nang Heritage Train
train: Restyled vintage coaches with saloon car (HD1–HD4)
highlight: Launched in 2024 on the 1906 colonial mainline, it eases over the Hai Van Pass with clouds above and fishing fleets directly below the rails.

name: Taksaka
train: KAI New Generation stainless-steel executive & luxury coaches
highlight: Java’s flagship day train links the capital and the sultan’s city in six hours, skirting volcanoes beyond Cirebon and Purwokerto.

name: Jiayang Steam Train
train: C2-class 762 mm steam locomotive, wooden open-window coaches
highlight: One of the world’s last everyday steam railways: C2 engines built for 1950s coal pits still haul villagers past rapeseed terraces for pocket change.

name: Jaffar Express
highlight: Pakistan’s longest through service tops the Bolan Pass at Kolpur (1,790 m), then follows the Indus corridor 1,632 km to the gate of the Khyber.

name: Royal Railway Northern Line
highlight: Cambodia’s 1932 French rice line, silenced by war and revived in 2018, ambles past Tonle Sap paddies to Battambang’s art-deco station.

name: Eurostar
highlight: The original Channel Tunnel service to the EU capital — St Pancras to Bruxelles-Midi in under two hours, city centre to city centre.

name: Eurostar
highlight: A single train from St Pancras to Amsterdam Centraal since 2018 — under the Channel, across Flanders and the Dutch polders in about four hours.

name: TGV / ICE
highlight: A Franco-German alliance since 2007: 320 km/h on the LGV Est to Lorraine, then the Saar and Rhine-Main into Frankfurt, TGV and ICE sharing the run.

name: Alvia
highlight: Off the 1992 Madrid–Seville high-speed spine, a gauge-changing Talgo carries on through sherry country to the Atlantic at Cádiz.

name: Borealis
highlight: Amtrak’s May 2024 daytime run up the Mississippi from Chicago through Milwaukee and Wisconsin’s river bluffs to the Twin Cities.

name: Aoniyoshi
highlight: A 2022 salon train styled on Nara’s Tenpyō court art, linking Osaka, the old capital Nara and Kyoto with a library bar and a sweets counter.

name: Nightjet
highlight: Relaunched in December 2023 after nine years dark: leave the Gare de l’Est after dinner, cross the Rhine in the night, wake in Berlin.

name: Snälltåget
highlight: A private summer sleeper down through Sweden, over the Öresund bridge past Copenhagen and across Denmark to Berlin — outlasting the state night trains.

name: Desert Express
train: TransNamib lounge & sleeper cars, GE diesels
highlight: A slow luxury run from the highland capital down the escarpment to the Atlantic, with dune walks as the Namib shifts from gold to coastal fog.

name: Wuppertal Schwebebahn
highlight: The world’s oldest electric suspension railway, hanging from a steel frame above the Wupper river since 1901 — a monorail that never stopped commuting.

name: Odontotos Rack Railway
highlight: An 1896 rack line claws 700 m up the sheer Vouraikos gorge, threading tunnels and clinging to ledges above the torrent to the town of Kalavryta.

name: Voralpen-Express
highlight: An hourly cross-country ride through the pre-Alps — the Wattwil moorland, the Rapperswil lake dam and the Rothenthurm high bog — linking two lake cities.

name: Cremallera de Montserrat
highlight: A rack railway climbing the serrated massif to the Benedictine monastery at 720 m — the pilgrims’ way to Catalonia’s black Madonna since 1892.

name: SL Yamaguchi
train: D51 steam with retro Series 35 4000 coaches
highlight: A pacific steam locomotive has hauled this special up the Yamaguchi line to the little castle town of Tsuwano, the “Kyoto of San-in”, since 1979.

name: Fairy Queen Express
train: 1855 Fairy Queen steam locomotive, heritage saloon
highlight: Hauled by the 1855 Fairy Queen, the world’s oldest working steam locomotive, on a heritage run from Delhi out to the tiger country around Alwar.

name: Chemin de fer de La Mure
highlight: Reopened in 2021, this cliffside metre-gauge line hangs on ledges and viaducts high above the turquoise Monteynard reservoir in the Isère.

name: Petit Train de la Rhune
highlight: A 1924 wooden cog train grinds up to the 905 m Basque summit of La Rhune — pottok ponies and griffon vultures alongside, the Bay of Biscay below.

name: Beijing–Tianjin Intercity
highlight: China’s first high-speed line, opened for the 2008 Olympics — 350 km/h across the North China Plain, capital to port city in half an hour.

name: Shanghai–Chengdu HSR
highlight: The great east–west artery, threading Nanjing, Wuhan and the Three Gorges country to reach Sichuan’s capital from the coast in a single day.

name: Hangzhou–Huangshan HSR
highlight: Dubbed China’s most beautiful high-speed line, it slips through tea hills and water towns from West Lake to the granite of Yellow Mountain.

name: Guiyang–Chongqing HSR
highlight: Almost entirely bridge and tunnel, this 2018 line vaults the karst gorges of Guizhou to join two of the mountain south-west’s biggest cities.

name: Lanzhou–Chongqing Railway
highlight: Twenty years in the building through the folded Qinling ranges — hundreds of tunnels that finally gave Gansu a fast way down to the Yangtze.

name: Talgo Sarajevo–Banja Luka
highlight: A Spanish Talgo leans through the Bosna gorge between Bosnia’s two capitals, past Zenica’s steelworks and down the green Vrbas valley.

name: Athens–Kalambaka
highlight: The line to the monasteries in the sky — up from Athens across the Thessalian plain to Kalambaka, beneath the sandstone pillars of Meteora.

name: Shosholoza Meyl Trans-Karoo
train: Class 6E electric & diesel with sleeper cars
highlight: The classic Trans-Karoo run — a night and a day across the semi-desert heart of South Africa from the Highveld gold city down to Table Bay.

name: BR Express
highlight: Botswana’s overnight sleeper, reborn in 2016, trundles up the eastern rim of the Kalahari between Gaborone and the mining town of Francistown.

name: Central Line
highlight: The German-built Central Line of 1914, a metre-gauge marathon from the Indian Ocean across the plateau to Kigoma on Lake Tanganyika.

name: Baku–Tbilisi–Kars
highlight: The Silk Road revived: opened in 2017, it closes the last gap from the Caspian across the Caucasus to Turkey — a rail bridge between Asia and Europe.

name: Grand Trunk Express
train: LHB sleeper & AC coaches, WAP-7 electric
highlight: Running since 1929, the “GT” hauls down the spine of India through Bhopal and Nagpur to Chennai — 2,180 km in a day and a half.

name: Mandalay–Lashio
train: Myanmar Railways diesel with upper-class coaches
highlight: The Shan hill line crawls up from Mandalay to inch across the Gokteik viaduct — a 1900 American-built steel trestle 100 m above the gorge.

name: Gotthard Panorama Express
train: SBB panorama coaches & lake paddle steamer
highlight: A paddle steamer up Lake Lucerne, then the 1882 Gotthard mountain line’s spiral tunnels and the Wassen church glimpsed three times on the climb south.

name: AVE Madrid–Oviedo
highlight: The 2023 Variante de Pajares pierced the Cantabrian wall with a 25 km base tunnel, cutting Asturias’ link to Madrid to about three hours.

name: Alvia Madrid–Badajoz
highlight: High speed reached long-isolated Extremadura in 2022, sweeping past the Roman city of Mérida to the Portuguese border at Badajoz.

name: Tren de la Muerte
highlight: Bolivia’s notorious “Death Train” rattles east from Santa Cruz through Jesuit mission country and the Chiquitano dry forest to the Brazilian frontier.

name: Bosfor Express
highlight: The overnight heir to the Orient Express, crossing the Thracian plain from Istanbul over the Bulgarian border to Sofia as the berths are made up.

name: Albula Railway
highlight: The UNESCO Albula line spirals through helical tunnels and sweeps over the six-arch Landwasser Viaduct — a curved stone bridge running straight into a cliff — on its climb to St. Moritz.

name: Montenvers Railway
highlight: A rack railway since 1908, climbing from Chamonix to a balcony above the Mer de Glace — France’s largest glacier — beneath the Drus and the Grandes Jorasses.

name: Mont-Blanc Tramway
highlight: France’s highest railway grinds up to the Nid d’Aigle at 2,372 m on the flank of Mont Blanc — the start of the climbers’ route to the summit.

name: Schynige Platte Railway
highlight: Vintage 1914 cars grind up an Abt rack to a 2,000 m ridge with the Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau lined up across the valley — and an alpine garden at the top.

name: Genoa–Casella Railway
highlight: A metre-gauge mountain tram that climbs straight out of central Genoa over three Apennine valleys to the village of Casella, sea views at its back.

name: Nevada Northern Railway
highlight: The best-preserved steam-era short line in America — the “Ghost Train of Old Ely” still runs its 1910 locomotives out of a National Historic Landmark copper-country yard.

name: Campos do Jordão Railway
highlight: Brazil’s highest railway climbs the Serra da Mantiqueira to the mountain resort of Campos do Jordão at 1,743 m, a metre-gauge line electrified since the 1920s.

name: Zig Zag Railway
highlight: A 19th-century engineering marvel in the Blue Mountains: trains reverse down a rock-cut Z of sandstone viaducts that once carried the main western line off the plateau.

name: Silver Star
highlight: Amtrak’s daily New York–Miami run via the Carolinas and a loop through Tampa — the East Coast’s classic snowbird train down to the Florida sun.

name: AVE Madrid–Murcia
highlight: High speed reached the Segura in December 2022, running Madrid to Murcia via Cuenca and Albacete on a new elevated approach into the palm-country capital.

name: Le Mastrou (Vivarais)
highlight: A metre-gauge steam survivor climbing the granite gorge of the Doux from the Rhône at Tournon to the chestnut country of Lamastre, hauled by 1903 Mallet engines.

name: Chiemsee-Bahn
highlight: The world’s oldest steam tram still in service, an 1887 green Krauss engine that shuttles down to the Chiemsee jetty for the boat to Ludwig II’s island palace.

name: Monte Generoso Railway
highlight: A rack line from the shore of Lake Lugano to a 1,704 m summit whose Mario Botta “Fiore di pietra” lookout takes in the Alps from Monte Rosa to the Apennines.

name: Great Central Railway
highlight: Britain’s only double-track heritage main line, where two steam expresses can pass at speed just as they did on the last main line built towards the Continent.

name: Keighley & Worth Valley Railway
highlight: The branch that starred in the 1970 film “The Railway Children”, steaming up the Brontë moors past Haworth to the mill village of Oxenhope.

name: Kingston Flyer
highlight: A vintage steam service on the old Southland line by Lake Wakatipu, its 1920s Ab Pacific and wooden cars a survivor of New Zealand’s golden age of rail.

name: Himsagar Express
highlight: From the Himalayan foothills at Jammu to the very tip of India at Kanyakumari — about 3,785 km through a dozen states, one of the country’s longest single journeys.

name: Frecciarossa Milan–Reggio Calabria
highlight: The red arrow’s longest domestic run — the length of Italy from Milan down the Tyrrhenian coast to the Strait of Messina at Reggio Calabria.

name: Nightjet Vienna–Rome
highlight: Board in Vienna after dinner, cross the Carinthian Alps and the Venetian plain by night, and wake rolling into Rome — a sleeper between two imperial capitals.

name: Aurora Winter Train
highlight: The winter run north across a frozen Alaska — flag-stopping for bush residents between Anchorage and Fairbanks, the northern lights often overhead.
